name: fly
description: The flyctl is a command-line interface for fly.io

subcommands.name: agent
subcommands.description: Commands that manage the Fly agent, a background process that manages flyctl wireguard connections

subcommands.name: daemon-start
subcommands.description: Run the Fly agent in the foreground

options.description: Generate a name for the app

subcommands.name: move
subcommands.description: Move an app to another organization

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Open browser to current deployed application

options.description: Display the Docker image reference of the release
Will issue a restart against each Machine even if there are errors. ( Machines only )
Restarts app without waiting for health checks. ( Machines only )
Log in with an email and password interactively

subcommands.description: Logs out the currently logged in user

subcommands.name: whoami
subcommands.description: Displays the users email address/service identity currently authenticated and in use

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Set of build time variables in the form of NAME=VALUE pairs. Can be specified multiple times
Set the target build stage to build if the Dockerfile has more than one stage
Return immediately instead of monitoring deployment progress
Path to a Dockerfile. Defaults to the Dockerfile in the working directory
Set of environment variables in the form of NAME=VALUE pairs. Can be specified multiple times
The image tag or ID to deploy
Image label to use when tagging and pushing to the fly registry. Defaults to "deployment-{timestamp}"
Only perform builds locally using the local docker daemon
Do not use the build cache when building the image
Push image to registry after build is complete
Perform builds on a remote builder instance instead of using the local docker daemon
The strategy for replacing running instances. Options are canary, rolling, bluegreen, or immediate. Default is canary, or rolling when max-per-region is set

subcommands.name: dig
subcommands.description: Make DNS requests against Fly.io's internal DNS server

options.description: Just print the answers, not DNS record details

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: The DOCTOR command allows you to debug your Fly environment

subcommands.name: diag
subcommands.description: Send diagnostic information about your applications back to Fly.io

options.description: Send diagnostics even if we can't find your local Fly.io app

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates the app's image to the latest available version. (Fly Postgres only)

options.description: Return immediately instead of monitoring update progress
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The region where the address should be allocated
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The region where the address should be allocated
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Use the configuration file if present without prompting
Path to a Dockerfile. Defaults to the Dockerfile in the working directory
Always generate a name for the app
The name of the new app
The organization that will own the app
Path to app code and where a fly.toml file will be saved
The region to launch the new app in
Perform builds remotely without using the local docker daemon
The name of the new machine
Force kill machine if it's running
Set of build time variables in the form of NAME=VALUE pairs. Can be specified multiple times
Only perform builds locally using the local docker daemon
Perform builds remotely without using the local docker daemon
Set the target build stage to build if the Dockerfile has more than one stage
Kind of CPU to use (shared, dedicated)
Path to a Dockerfile. Defaults to the Dockerfile in the working directory
Set of environment variables in the form of NAME=VALUE pairs. Can be specified multiple times
Image label to use when tagging and pushing to the fly registry. Defaults to "deployment-{timestamp}"
Memory (in megabytes) to attribute to the machine
Machine name, will be generated if missing
Do not use the cache when building the image
The organization that will own the app
Preset guest cpu and memory for a machine
Volumes to mount in the form of <volume_id_or_name>:/path/inside/machine[:<options>]

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show current status of a running machine

options.description: Signal to stop the machine with (default: SIGINT)
Seconds to wait before killing the machine

subcommands.description: Move an app to another organization

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update an organization's remote builder image

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a user from an organization

subcommands.name: ping
subcommands.description: Test connectivity with ICMP ping messages

options.description: Size of probe to send (not including headers)

subcommands.name: attach
subcommands.description: Attach a postgres cluster to an app

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Database to use, defaults to a new database with the same name as the app
The database user to create, defaults to creating a user with the same name as the consuming app
The postgres cluster to attach to the app
The env variable name that will be added to the app. Defaults to DATABASE_URL
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The postgres database to connect to
The postgres user to connect with
The size of the initial postgres cluster
The name of the new app
The organization that will own the app
The superuser password. one will be generated for you if you leave this blank
The region to launch the new app in
Creates the volume with the contents of the snapshot
The size in GB for volumes
Path to an app config file or directory containing one

subcommands.name: detach
subcommands.description: Detach a postgres cluster from an app

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The postgres cluster to detach from the app
Path to an app config file or directory containing one

subcommands.name: proxy
subcommands.description: Proxies connections to a fly VM

options.description: Prompt to select from available instances from the current application

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Allow the app to run in the provided regions

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The process group to add the region to

subcommands.name: backup
subcommands.description: Sets the backup region pool with provided regions

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Shows the list of regions the app is allowed to run in

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Prevent the app from running in the provided regions

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The process group to remove the region from

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets the region pool with provided regions

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The process group to set regions for
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Display the Docker image reference of the release

subcommands.name: count
subcommands.description: Change an app's VM count to the given value

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
Max number of VMs per region
Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The process group to apply the memory size to
Path to an app config file or directory containing one

subcommands.name: vm
subcommands.description: Change an app's VM to a named size (eg. shared-cpu-1x, dedicated-cpu-1x, dedicated-cpu-2x...)

options.description: Path to an app config file or directory containing one
The process group to apply the VM size to
Memory in MB for the VM

subcommands.description: Connect to a running instance of the current app

Path to an app config file or directory containing one

subcommands.description: Create a root SSH certificate for your organization

options.description: Store keys in ~/.ssh, like normal keys
Overwrite existing SSH keys in same location, if we generated them
Keep the app directory after deployment

The organization that will own the app

subcommands.name: wg
subcommands.description: Commands that manage WireGuard peer connections

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Reset WireGuard peer connection for an organization

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Get status a WireGuard peer connection

subcommands.name: token
subcommands.description: Commands that managed WireGuard delegated access tokens

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a WireGuard token; token is name:<name> or token:<token>

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start a new WireGuard peer connection associated with a token (set FLY_WIREGUARD_TOKEN)

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Rekey a WireGuard peer connection associated with a token (set FLY_WIREGUARD_TOKEN)

subcommands.name: websockets
subcommands.description: Enable or disable WireGuard tunneling over WebSockets
